title: I would like to integrate Quickbooks Timesheets with Monday
excerpt: I will be using Monday mainly for project management, and I would like it to pull the hours worked data from Quickbooks Timesheets rather than having to manually enter it in. Is this possible?

posts.author: NicoleRossmerica (Nicole)
posts.cookedHtml: I will be using Monday mainly for project management, and I would like it to pull the hours worked data from Quickbooks Timesheets rather than having to manually enter it in. Is this possible?

posts.author: sachinkarma (sachin kumar karma)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @NicoleRossmerica

Sounds like a great idea to streamline your workflows.

Here’s how I’d approach integrating QuickBooks Timesheets with Monday.com to pull in hours worked automatically and eliminate manual entry:

I’ll set up an automation using Make.com or Zapier to sync time logs. The process will trigger whenever timesheets are updated in QuickBooks, and the data will flow directly into relevant Monday boards—keeping everything up-to-date without any manual effort.

posts.author: TrueParallel (Mark Rosenberg)
posts.cookedHtml: What I would lik eto do is to be able to enter time in monday and integrate that with QB time so all in one application. I use QB time now to do time entry monday for project management but would like to track time in Monday and push back into QB Desktop for billing but curently track time in QB time (formerly t-sheets) and that integrates/synchs with QB desktop (NOT QB ONLINE). So if I can get qb time and monday to play nicely, then my problem solved but can’t find any direct time connection with QB desktop only online.
